subject: Use policy-pdpd-cl image for drools
commit_message:

Patch Set 1:

Are you ready for this to be reviewed?

Patch Set 1:

Thanks Pam, not yet, waiting to retest with some changes in the image I have pending to test.   I'll put it out later today for inspection, once I finish the other changes.

I'm also entertaining the idea of moving this "official" docker compose under its own directory, for example "docker-compose" or "orchestration" and leave the older one as is until we clean up its usage from the legacy csit?    Do you have an opinion about it?

Patch Set 1:

> Thanks Pam, not yet, waiting to retest with some changes in the
 > image I have pending to test.   I'll put it out later today for
 > inspection, once I finish the other changes.
 > 
 > I'm also entertaining the idea of moving this "official" docker
 > compose under its own directory, for example "docker-compose" or
 > "orchestration" and leave the older one as is until we clean up its
 > usage from the legacy csit?    Do you have an opinion about it?

The legacy CSIT don't use this file, it uses the docker-compose-integration.yml. Which I would like to delete and use the one that is housed in the CSIT code repo. I copied it over in my last CSIT review. So go ahead and make changes here.

Patch Set 1:

If I recall correctly since it does a clone, it uses the config/ files underneath ..  and  many of them are removed.   So the issue, is that if I keep it here, I need to deal with the legacy csit test too.   That's the issue.

Patch Set 1:

> If I recall correctly since it does a clone, it uses the config/
 > files underneath ..  and  many of them are removed.   So the issue,
 > is that if I keep it here, I need to deal with the legacy csit test
 > too.   That's the issue.

Ok yes, you are right. Now I'm thinking that maybe we want all the compose scripts here and have the CSIT clone this repo. Otherwise, changes will get out of synch.
Patch Set 2: Published edit on patch set 1.

Patch Set 2:

I think this could be merged - I undid the deletion of files under config/ directory, so to not conflict with the csit until that's cleaned up.   Once that's cleaned up, can delete unused files with new drools-applications image.    The images are set to use the latest snapshot from dublin, instead of no tag which implies latest that sometimes was pointing to older casablanca releases, the latest was a little unpredictible.
